::: {.content-visible when-format="pdf"}
```{=latex}
\small
\begin{multicols}{2}
\raggedright
```
:::

### Module 1: Combinatorial Fundamentals

#### Week 1: Fundamentals of Square-Logic

+ **Tuesday:** *The classical $n$-cube.* §[-@sec-hypersquares-fundamental-phenomena] and §3. 
  + Syllabus discussion,
  + Face lattices and binary string representations.

+ **Thursday:** *Combinatorial topologies.* §2.2--2.3. 
  + Incidence data and bounded half-spaces,
  + Moving from geometric to combinatorial viewpoints,
  + Where classical intuitions fail.

+ **Homework 0** by Friday at 4 PM.
+ **Journal 1** by Saturday at midnight.

#### Week 2: Transport Operators

+ **Tuesday:** *Discrete transport.* §4.1--4.2.
  + Oriented edges and operator assignment,
  + Defining the hypersquare space,
  + Sub-cubes and examples.

+ **Thursday:** *Coherence conditions.* §4.3--4.4.
  + 2-face automorphisms,
  + Introduction to `SageMath` for hyper-dimensional grids.

+ **Homework 1** due by Wednesday at 4 PM.
+ **Journal 2** due by Saturday at midnight.

#### Week 3: The Departure from Riemann

+ **Tuesday:** *Local Euclidean charts.* §5.1.
  + Where the Pythagorean theorem still holds,
  + Properties of discrete orthogonality.

+ **Thursday:** *Global incompatibility.* §5.2 and §6.1.
  + The breakdown of metric tensors across boundaries,
  + Uniqueness of local limits.

+ **Homework 2** due by Tuesday at 4 PM.
+ **Journal 3** due by Saturday at midnight.

::: {.content-visible when-format="pdf"}
```{=latex}
\vfill\null
\columnbreak
```
:::

### Module 2: Holonomy & Anomalous Geodesics

#### Week 4: Parallel Transport

+ **Tuesday:** *Phase shifts.* §6.2--6.3.
  + Transport across non-trivial loops,
  + Polynomial invariants of discrete paths.

+ **Thursday:** *Holonomy of hypersquares.* §6.4--6.5.
  + Calculations without smooth manifolds,
  + Coherence as invariance.
  + **Begin Midterm 1.**

+ **Homework 3** due by Tuesday at 4 PM.
+ **Journal 4** due by Saturday at midnight.

#### Week 5: Square-Logic Calculus

+ **Tuesday:** *Discrete actions.* §7.1.
  + Minimizing edge sequences,
  + Projections across boundaries.

+ **Thursday:** *Anomalous geodesics.* §7.2.
  + Periodicity in straight lines,
  + Redefining shortest paths.

+ **Homework 4** due by Tuesday at 4 PM.
+ **Midterm 1** due in class on Thursday.
+ **Journal 5** due by Saturday at midnight.

#### Week 6: Path Intersections

+ **Tuesday:** *Paradoxical topologies.* §8.1.
  + Self-intersecting geodesics without degeneracy.

+ **Thursday:** *Applications.* §8.2.
  + **Begin Midterm Project (The Great Geodesic Hunt).**

+ **Homework 5** due by Tuesday at 4 PM.
+ **Journal 6** due by Saturday at midnight.

#### Week 7: The Chessboard Universe

+ **Tuesday:** *Four-dimensional modeling.* §9.1.
  + Grid-based continuous structures,
  + Random walks on $n$-dimensional chessboards.

+ **Thursday:** *Periodic transitions.* §9.2.
  + Macro-scale anomalies,
  + Shuffling dimensions.

+ **Homework 6** due by Tuesday at 4 PM.
+ **Journal 7** due by Saturday at midnight.

### Module 3: Applied Hypersquares

#### Week 8: Data Compression

+ **Tuesday:** *Review.* §9.3.
  + Wrapping up 4D anomalies,
  + Accounting of our progress.

+ **Thursday:** *Topological storage.* §10.1--10.3.
  + How non-metric structures compress information,
  + Bounding infinite sequences.
  + **Begin Midterm 2.**

+ **Homework 7** due by Tuesday at 4 PM.
+ **Midterm Project** due by Thursday at 4 PM.
+ **Journal 8** due by Saturday at midnight.

#### Week 9: Infinite Complexes

+ **Tuesday:** *Lifting to infinity.* §10.4 and §11.1.
  + Infinite cubical complexes,
  + Exponentials and path lifting.

+ **Thursday:** *Continuous limits.* §11.2--11.3.
  + Approximating spheres with hypersquares,
  + Irreducible boundary faces.

+ **Homework 8** due by Tuesday at 4 PM.
+ **Midterm 2** due in class on Thursday.
+ **Journal 9** due by Saturday at midnight.

#### Week 10: Further Topics

+ **Tuesday:** *Advanced anomalies.* §12.1, §12.3--12.4.
  + Connections to fractal geometry and continuous nowhere-differentiable manifolds.

+ **Thursday:** *Wrapping up.* §12.5.
  + Review, and where to go next.
  + **Begin Final Exam.**

+ **Homework 9** due by Tuesday at 4 PM.

::: {.content-visible when-format="pdf"}
```{=latex}
\vfill\null
\columnbreak
```
:::

### Final Exam Period

#### Week 11:

+ **Review Sessions**
+ [**Final Exam**](https://www.carleton.edu/registrar/academic-term/exams/) due 2026-03-16 by 4 PM.

::: {.content-visible when-format="pdf"}
```{=latex}
\end{multicols}
\normalsize
```
:::
